comments.author: @sahmed9583
comments.text:
A multidimensional documentary. More positive than negative.  Factual. Unfortunately, political instabilities and military coups remain on the darker side. As a Pakistani 🇵🇰 common man, we want to rise. We want to be appreciated and want to have the same respect and dignity that we deserve. We are hard working, brave and talented nation.
 👏  well done. The documentary is great.

replies.author: @WhereonEarth4K
replies.text: Thank you! hearing this from a Pakistani means a great deal to us. The hard work, courage, and artistry of your people are the heart of the story we wanted to tell. The challenges Pakistan navigates are real, but so is the dignity and warmth that comes through in everyday life there. May the world come to see Pakistan the way Pakistanis already see it. 🙏

comments.author: @abdulqudussaggu3222
comments.text: Absolutely breathtaking! The 4K clarity is so hyper-realistic and pristine that it genuinely looks otherworldly. The cinematography effortlessly captures the sheer scale of Northern Pakistan's peaks and the stunning turquoise of Attabad Lake. This isn't just a travel video—it’s a masterclass in visual storytelling that deserves to be recognized at the Travel Video Awards or the CIFFT World Tourism Film Awards. The cinematographer deserves a grand jury prize already!

comments.author: @arifsaleem5467
comments.text:
31:25
The 1971 conflict is often presented in an oversimplified way, as if a small Pakistani force single-handedly killed millions of civilians. In reality, the total Pakistani military presence in East Pakistan was limited to only around 40000, with only 23000 being frontline combat troops, while the conflict itself was a complex civil war involving the Mukti Bahini, pro-Pakistan Urdu speaking militias, communal violence, reprisals, and later direct Indian military intervention. Even many researchers who reject the official 3 to 6 million figure coupled with a senseless accusation of over a million rape cases, though still acknowledge large-scale atrocities, but they also argue that attributing all that solely to the Pakistani Army is a baseless accusation not supported by clear demographic or forensic evidence.
Also the mass murders of Urdu speaking community by mukti bahini and Indian army was totally neglected.
